Title: TxtH is a key component of the thaxtomin biosynthetic machinery in the potato common scab pathogen Streptomyces scabies

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extract; produced by bacterial mycelium
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential pathogenicity determinant for potato common scab disease development; contributes to plant tissue necrosis and virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of txtH significantly reduced thaxtomin A production and reduced virulence; complementation partially restored thaxtomin A production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation/overexpression with ermEp*; co-expression with TxtA/TxtB adenylation domains in E. coli; western blot of soluble fractions; HPLC quantification; LC-HRESIMS; potato tuber slice bioassay; RT-PCR
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TxtA adenylation domain; TxtB adenylation domain
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TxtH promotes soluble expression of TxtA and TxtB adenylation domains, supporting a chaperone-like role in thaxtomin NRPS assembly/function. 2. Deletion of txtH significantly reduces thaxtomin A and causes accumulation of thaxtomin B and D intermediates, with partial restoration by txtH complementation. 3. Loss of txtH reduces virulence on potato tuber tissue, linking thaxtomin biosynthesis to pathogenicity.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions with cellobiose/oat bran media; potato tuber slice infection assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antimicrobial target for reducing thaxtomin A production and potato common scab virulence by disrupting MLP-supported NRPS function.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: txtH deletion caused reduced txtC transcription likely due to polar effects and accumulation of intermediates thaxtomin B and D.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TxtH function is species/pathway specific: S. scabies MLPs can replace it in vivo when overexpressed, but non-cognate cdaX and clav_p1293 cannot; conserved residues N17, Q21, S23, L24, W25, W35, W55, T56, D57 are important for solubility-promoting activity.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extract; produced by bacterial mycelium
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential pathogenicity determinant for potato common scab disease development; contributes to virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of mlp_scab together with txtH abolished detectable thaxtomin A production; overexpression in the triple MLP mutant restored thaxtomin A production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: double/triple mutant analysis; overexpression complementation with ermEp*; co-expression with TxtA/TxtB adenylation domains in E. coli; western blot; HPLC; potato tuber slice bioassay; RT-PCR
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TxtA adenylation domain; TxtB adenylation domain
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mlp_scab can functionally replace TxtH when overexpressed, restoring thaxtomin A production in the triple MLP-deficient strain. 2. Loss of mlp_scab in a txtH deletion background abolishes thaxtomin A biosynthesis, indicating critical compensatory function. 3. MLPscab promotes soluble expression of TxtA and TxtB adenylation domains in E. coli, although it is less efficient for TxtB than TxtH.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions with cellobiose/oat bran media; potato tuber slice infection assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to block compensatory MLP cross-talk and suppress thaxtomin A-mediated virulence.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acts in scabichelin-associated context natively; pathway crosstalk may complicate selective targeting.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in the NRPS gene cluster for the siderophore scabichelin; promotes A-domain solubility but in vivo replacement of TxtH depends on engineered overexpression.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extract; produced by bacterial mycelium
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: essential pathogenicity determinant for potato common scab disease development; contributes to virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Single deletion of mlp_lipo did not alter thaxtomin A production, but overexpression in the triple MLP mutant restored thaxtomin A production, showing functional replacement of TxtH.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single/double/triple mutant analysis; overexpression complementation with ermEp*; co-expression with TxtA/TxtB adenylation domains in E. coli; western blot; HPLC; RT-PCR
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TxtA adenylation domain; TxtB adenylation domain
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mlp_lipo is not required alone for thaxtomin A production in wild-type S. scabies. 2. When overexpressed, mlp_lipo restores thaxtomin A production in the triple MLP mutant, demonstrating cross-pathway functional replacement of TxtH. 3. MLPlipo promotes soluble expression of TxtA and TxtB adenylation domains in E. coli.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions with cellobiose/oat bran media
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May inform strategies to understand or disrupt MLP redundancy in NRPS toxin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native function likely linked to a putative lipopeptide pathway, so perturbation may affect additional metabolites.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded within a putative lipopeptide NRPS cluster; shares only ~52.3% amino acid identity with TxtH yet can replace it when engineered.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nonribosomal peptide synthetase (NRPS) with adenylation domain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selects and activates the appropriate amino acid substrate during thaxtomin biosynthesis as part of NRPS assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid substrate for thaxtomin assembly
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: activated aminoacyl intermediate in thaxtomin NRPS assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TxtA adenylation domain requires TxtH or related S. scabies MLPs for soluble expression, indicating dependence of thaxtomin biosynthesis on MLP-assisted NRPS function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous co-expression in E. coli ybdZ mutant; western blot of soluble fractions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extract; produced by bacterial mycelium
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pathogenicity determinant causing potato common scab symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential toxin biosynthesis target, though not directly manipulated in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions with cellobiose/oat bran media
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary subject only insofar as its A-domain solubility was tested; not directly genetically manipulated as focal causal gene for metabolite change in vivo.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nonribosomal peptide synthetase (NRPS) with adenylation domain
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Selects and activates the appropriate amino acid substrate during thaxtomin biosynthesis as part of NRPS assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid substrate for thaxtomin assembly
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: activated aminoacyl intermediate in thaxtomin NRPS assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TxtB adenylation domain requires TxtH or related S. scabies MLPs for soluble expression, indicating dependence of thaxtomin biosynthesis on MLP-assisted NRPS function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous co-expression in E. coli ybdZ mutant; western blot of soluble fractions
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: culture extract; produced by bacterial mycelium
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pathogenicity determinant causing potato common scab symptoms
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential toxin biosynthesis target, though not directly manipulated in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions with cellobiose/oat bran media
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TxtB solubility was generally more sensitive than TxtA to some TxtH point mutations and to weaker support by MLPscab; not directly manipulated in vivo as focal causal gene.
